title: How Plants Make Food
content: Photosynthesis is the process plants use to convert sunlight into food. Inside the leaves of a plant are tiny structures called chloroplasts that contain a green pigment called chlorophyll. Using sunlight, water absorbed from the soil, and carbon dioxide from the air, the plant produces glucose — a sugar it uses for energy and growth. As a bonus, oxygen is released as a by-product, which is exactly the air we breathe.
